packageRules.description: Mirror the prior Dependabot commit convention: `deps(nuget): ...` for NuGet bumps and the TUnit version-literal sync via customManagers (both resolve to the nuget datasource).

Override for `dotnet-sdk` (tracked via global.json). Renovate's nuget manager surfaces it, but it is the SDK itself, not a NuGet package; tag it as a CI/tooling concern with its own scope so PR titles read `ci(dotnet-sdk): ...`.
Mirror the prior Dependabot commit convention: `ci(github-actions): ...` for workflow action bumps.

Group the three TUnit packages (TUnit, TUnit.Assertions, TUnit.Core) into a single PR. They share a source repo and release in lockstep, so a single PR keeps Directory.Packages.props and the lock files internally consistent and avoids three partial-bump PRs per TUnit release.

Auto-merge non-major updates (patch, minor, digest, pin, pinDigest, lockFileMaintenance) once CI passes (requires 'Allow auto-merge' to be enabled on the repo). Major bumps stay manual. Digest is included because Renovate tracks SHA-pinned GitHub Actions as updateType 'digest' (not a semver category).

customManagers.description: Keep TUnit version literals (root README Requirements line, package README Requirements line, smoketest csproj PackageReference, bug-report Issue Form placeholder) in lockstep with the central TUnit package version. Renovate updates these in the same PR that bumps Directory.Packages.props.
